response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and backend software engineer with 14+ years of experience, which technically satisfies the BS+8yr education threshold. However, the role demands highly specialized hardware engineering skills — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, and SIMD/MIMD design — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. His closest adjacent experience is low-latency software development using async I/O and DLang at Sociomantic, but this is firmly in the software layer and does not transfer to hardware-software co-design or chip-level performance analysis. There is no evidence of deep learning, neural network optimization, or any hardware architecture background.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience across diverse domains, satisfying the BS+8yr qualification threshold
Low-latency systems work (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, high-performance databases) shows performance-aware engineering mindset
Polyglot background (DLang, TypeScript, C/C++, Java) and embedded/hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, NI LabWindows CVI) indicates some breadth beyond pure web development
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in AI, deep learning, neural network training or inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD design
All meaningful experience is at the application software layer; no hardware-software co-design, chip performance analysis, or cloud infrastructure for AI accelerators

key_factors.factor: No AI/deep learning or GPU background
